deep_dive.title: FlashMemory-DeepSeek-V4: Lightning Index Ultra-Long Context via Lookahead Sparse Attention
deep_dive.abstract: Conventional LLMs keep the full KV cache loaded during decoding, causing a severe GPU memory bottleneck for ultra-long context serving. In this report, we propose Lookahead Sparse Attention (LSA), a novel inference paradigm powered by a Neural Memory Indexer built upon the DeepSeek-V4 architecture. Rather than passively attending to all historical tokens, LSA proactively predicts future context demands and preserves only the query-critical KV chunks in the GPU memory. Crucially, we instantiate this architecture via a backbone-free decoupled training strategy. By formulating the indexer as a standard dual-encoder architecture, we train it independently using standard retrieval training frameworks without ever loading the massive backbone model into GPU memory.   We demonstrate that this "less is more" paradigm significantly maximizes serving efficiency while acting as an effective attention denoiser in tasks that rely on long-term global memory. Across primary long-context evaluation suites (e.g., LongBench-v2, LongMemEval, and RULER), FM-DS-V4 compresses the average physical KV cache footprint down to merely 13.5% of the full-context baseline, while consistently preserving or slightly elevating downstream accuracy (+0.6% absolute margin on average). Crucially, at extreme 500K scales, FlashMemory suppresses the physical KV cache overhead by over 90% without destabilizing the backbone's core reasoning capacities.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: lookahead sparse attention with neural memory indexer for long-context acceleration
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于神经内存索引器的前瞻稀疏注意力实现长上下文加速
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于神经内存索引器的前瞻稀疏注意力实现长上下文加速
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于神经内存索引器的前瞻稀疏注意力实现长上下文加速
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Lookahead Sparse Attention (LSA) with a neural memory indexer to predict future context demands and keep only critical KV chunks in GPU memory, enabling ultra-long context serving.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 传统LLM在解码时保留全部KV缓存，成为超长上下文的GPU内存瓶颈。本文提出前瞻稀疏注意力（LSA），基于神经内存索引器主动预测未来上下文需求，仅保留关键KV块在GPU内存中。通过无骨干的解耦训练，索引器可独立训练，无需加载大模型。该方法在超长上下文场景下显著降低内存占用并加速推理。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 传统LLM在解码时保留全部KV缓存，成为超长上下文的GPU内存瓶颈。本文提出前瞻稀疏注意力（LSA），基于神经内存索引器主动预测未来上下文需求，仅保留关键KV块在GPU内存中。通过无骨干的解耦训练，索引器可独立训练，无需加载大模型。该方法在超长上下文场景下显著降低内存占用并加速推理。

deep_dive.title: IntentKV: Cross-Turn Intent-Aware KV Cache Pruning for Agent Inference
deep_dive.abstract: Multi-turn LLM agents fan short queries into long trajectories of tool calls, search results, and intermediate reasoning. Both KV memory and KV read bandwidth grow by orders of magnitude across a single trajectory, making the key-value (KV) cache, not parameter compute, the dominant serving bottleneck for long-horizon agents. We introduce IntentKV, learned KV pruning that keeps the base LLM frozen. IntentKV maintains a session-level QueryMemory of cross-turn intent, scores live history tokens with a memory-attention rule, and adds a zero-initialized residual head with cross-attention over current-query K-vectors. To stay composable with prefix caches, eviction is a slot-map redirection: dropped positions route to a sentinel dead slot while surviving K/V rows, RoPE phases, and slot identities stay in place. IntentKV matches the no-pruning full-cache baseline with almost no accuracy drop under tight KV budgets: at an 8k KV budget, mean peak request tokens drop 23.9% on Qwen3-8B and 30.7% on Qwen2.5-14B. On the 100 longest BCP queries that all methods complete on Qwen2.5-14B, IntentKV-8k further cuts worst-case peak request tokens from 92.3k to 20.5k, a 77.8% reduction, and worst-case raw KV reads from 411M to 31M, a 92.6% reduction.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: KV cache pruning for multi-turn agents
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向多轮代理的KV缓存剪枝
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向多轮代理的KV缓存剪枝
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向多轮代理的KV缓存剪枝
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: IntentKV performs learned KV pruning guided by cross-turn intent, reducing KV cache size while maintaining agent performance.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 多轮LLM代理中KV缓存成为主要瓶颈。IntentKV通过维护会话级查询记忆，对历史token进行意图感知评分，并用零初始化残差头处理当前查询。剪枝采用槽位映射重定向以兼容前缀缓存。实验表明在保持代理性能的同时大幅减少KV缓存占用。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 多轮LLM代理中KV缓存成为主要瓶颈。IntentKV通过维护会话级查询记忆，对历史token进行意图感知评分，并用零初始化残差头处理当前查询。剪枝采用槽位映射重定向以兼容前缀缓存。实验表明在保持代理性能的同时大幅减少KV缓存占用。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: KV cache reuse in large language model inference

deep_dive.title: Prefilling-dLLM: Predictive Prefilling for Long-Context Inference in Diffusion Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: Diffusion large language models (dLLMs) re-encode the entire prefix at every denoising step, causing recomputation that scales   quadratically with context length and becomes prohibitive for long-context scenarios. We propose Prefilling-dLLM, a training-free   prefill-decode disaggregation framework for dLLMs that partitions the prefix into N chunks, caches their KV representations once,   and selects the top-K most relevant chunks with intra-chunk token sparsity for decoding, showing that sparse prefilling can   outperform dense attention while reducing per-step complexity from quadratic in the full sequence length to quadratic only in the   decode length. On LongBench and InfiniteBench, Prefilling-dLLM achieves state-of-the-art quality among dLLM acceleration methods,   and an attention kernel that parallelizes decoding over the non-contiguously cached chunk KV yields 9.1--28.0x speedup at 8K--32K   contexts. We further show that beginning-of-sequence tokens prepended to each chunk act as periodic attention anchors that eliminate   the lost-in-the-middle phenomenon. Code is available at https://github.com/menik1126/Prefilling-dLLM.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: prefix caching with chunk-level KV reuse for long-context inference acceleration
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过分块级KV缓存和稀疏预填充加速长上下文推理
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过分块级KV缓存和稀疏预填充加速长上下文推理
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过分块级KV缓存和稀疏预填充加速长上下文推理
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Prefilling-dLLM, a training-free framework that caches KV representations of prefix chunks and selects top-K relevant chunks with intra-chunk sparsity, reducing per-step complexity from quadratic in full sequence to quadratic in decode length, achieving state-of-the-art on LongBench and InfiniteBench.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 扩散语言模型在长上下文推理中面临每步重新编码整个前缀导致二次复杂度的瓶颈。本文提出Prefilling-dLLM，一种无需训练的预填充-解码分离框架，将前缀分块并一次性缓存KV表示，解码时选择最相关的K块并结合块内令牌稀疏性，使每步复杂度从全序列二次降至仅解码长度二次，在LongBench和InfiniteBench上达到最先进水平，证明了稀疏预填充可超越密集注意力。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 扩散语言模型在长上下文推理中面临每步重新编码整个前缀导致二次复杂度的瓶颈。本文提出Prefilling-dLLM，一种无需训练的预填充-解码分离框架，将前缀分块并一次性缓存KV表示，解码时选择最相关的K块并结合块内令牌稀疏性，使每步复杂度从全序列二次降至仅解码长度二次，在LongBench和InfiniteBench上达到最先进水平，证明了稀疏预填充可超越密集注意力。

deep_dive.title: Express Language Modeling
deep_dive.abstract: We introduce a new tool, Express, for converting a non-causal attention approximation into a causal approximation with matching approximation guarantees. When combined with the state-of-the-art Thinformer approximation, Express improves upon the best known causal attention guarantees, delivering $\log^{3/2}(n)/s$ approximation error with only $O(s)$ memory and $O(s^2 \log^2(n))$ compression overhead for a sequence of length $n$. We pair these developments with an efficient I/O-aware Triton implementation, demonstrate substantial speedups over FlashAttention 2, and use Express to overcome four resource bottlenecks in the language modeling pipeline: long-context prefill, KV cache compression, long-form memory-constrained decoding, and long-form compute-constrained decoding.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: attention approximation conversion for KV cache compression and long-context prefill
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 注意力近似转换用于KV缓存压缩和长上下文预填充
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 注意力近似转换用于KV缓存压缩和长上下文预填充
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 注意力近似转换用于KV缓存压缩和长上下文预填充
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Express converts non-causal attention approximations to causal ones with matching guarantees, achieving KV cache compression and long-context speedups with I/O-aware implementation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: LLM推理面临长上下文预填充、KV缓存压缩等瓶颈。Express提供一种工具，将非因果注意力近似转换为因果近似，并保持近似保证。结合Thinformer，在O(s)内存下实现log^(3/2)(n)/s近似误差，并配有高效I/O感知Triton实现。实验表明Express在多个任务上超越FlashAttention 2，有效解决长上下文和内存受限解码问题。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: LLM推理面临长上下文预填充、KV缓存压缩等瓶颈。Express提供一种工具，将非因果注意力近似转换为因果近似，并保持近似保证。结合Thinformer，在O(s)内存下实现log^(3/2)(n)/s近似误差，并配有高效I/O感知Triton实现。实验表明Express在多个任务上超越FlashAttention 2，有效解决长上下文和内存受限解码问题。

deep_dive.title: RKSC: Reasoning-Aware KV Cache Sharing and Confident Early Exit for Multi-Step LLM Inference
deep_dive.abstract: We introduce RKSC (Reasoning-Aware KV Cache Sharing), a training-free inference framework that eliminates two structural redundancies in multi-branch LLM reasoning pipelines. ASKS (Attention-Similarity KV Sharing) computes the prefix KV cache once and broadcasts it to all semantically similar branches via hidden-state cosine similarity, strictly generalising the token-exact prefix caching used by vLLM and SGLang. CGEE (Confidence-Gated Early Exit) applies two complementary exit mechanisms: (1) it skips the verification forward pass entirely when generation confidence is decisive across branches, and (2) it terminates the verification pass at an intermediate layer when per-layer entropy stabilises, using lightweight hooks on the transformer backbone. RSBCM (Reasoning-Selective Block Cache Manager) prevents unbounded cache growth via attention-weighted depth-priority eviction. Across five model families (7B-10B), four benchmarks, and 1,000 evaluated problems, RKSC achieves a mean speedup of 3.008x over the No-KV baseline (peak 3.990x), a 1.66x mean improvement over vLLM-equivalent prefix caching, with a CGEE-induced error rate of only 0.37% (6 errors out of 1,616 verify calls). No fine-tuning or architecture changes are required. Code is available at https://github.com/AnirudhSekar/RKSC.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: generalizes token-exact prefix caching to shared prefix among semantic-similar branches
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过语义相似度将精确前缀缓存泛化为可共享前缀，实现KV缓存复用
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过语义相似度将精确前缀缓存泛化为可共享前缀，实现KV缓存复用
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过语义相似度将精确前缀缓存泛化为可共享前缀，实现KV缓存复用
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: RKSC introduces attention-similarity-based KV sharing and confidence-gated early exit for multi-branch LLM reasoning, eliminating redundant computation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出RKSC，一个无需训练的多分支LLM推理加速框架。其中ASKS通过隐藏状态余弦相似度计算语义相似分支的前缀KV缓存，实现一次计算、多次广播，严格推广了vLLM和SGLang的精确前缀缓存。CGEE则通过置信度门控早停减少验证前向传递。实验表明，RKSC在保持推理质量的前提下显著降低了延迟和计算量，为推理流水线的冗余消除提供了新方法。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文提出RKSC，一个无需训练的多分支LLM推理加速框架。其中ASKS通过隐藏状态余弦相似度计算语义相似分支的前缀KV缓存，实现一次计算、多次广播，严格推广了vLLM和SGLang的精确前缀缓存。CGEE则通过置信度门控早停减少验证前向传递。实验表明，RKSC在保持推理质量的前提下显著降低了延迟和计算量，为推理流水线的冗余消除提供了新方法。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: prefix caching techniques for LLM inference

deep_dive.title: Parallel Causal Associative Fields: Gated Sparse Memory for Long-Context Language Modeling
deep_dive.abstract: Transformers achieve strong language modeling performance by providing direct token-to-token communication paths, but causal self-attention scales quadratically with context length. Recurrent and state-space models reduce this cost, yet compress history into sequentially updated fixed-size states. This paper studies a third primitive: a parallel content-addressed memory over causal successor records. The proposed Parallel Causal Associative Field (PCAF) writes local records from a context window into hash buckets, retrieves a bounded candidate set for the current query, forms a sparse cache distribution over successor tokens, and mixes that cache with a parametric local language model through a learned gate. The resulting model maintains sparse long-context access while avoiding a single fixed recurrent state bottleneck. We evaluate PCAF under full autoregressive pretraining on WikiText-103 and PG-19 using a distributed Google Cloud TPU v4-32 pod. At 303M parameters and context length T = 2048, PCAF-semantic reaches 36.31 perplexity on WikiText-103 and 52.45 perplexity on PG-19, compared with 47.49 and 53.84 for a matched dense Transformer. PCAF-semantic simultaneously processes 0.61-0.62M tokens/s across the TPU pod, versus 0.43M tokens/s for dense and local attention baselines. Supporting 41M-parameter multi-seed sweeps and single-GPU component ablations show that the associative cache, retrieval capacity, and learned gate materially affect the speed-quality trade-off.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: parallel content-addressed sparse cache for long-context language modeling
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于长上下文语言建模的并行内容寻址稀疏缓存
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于长上下文语言建模的并行内容寻址稀疏缓存
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于长上下文语言建模的并行内容寻址稀疏缓存
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Parallel Causal Associative Fields propose a gated sparse memory that writes local records into hash buckets and retrieves a sparse cache distribution, reducing quadratic attention cost while maintaining language modeling performance on long contexts.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: Transformer在长上下文上二次缩放问题严重。该论文提出并行因果关联域（PCAF），通过并行内容寻址记忆机制，将上下文窗口中的局部记录写入哈希桶，为当前查询检索有限候选集，形成稀疏缓存分布，并与参数化语言模型通过门控混合。在长序列语言建模基准上，PCAF在保持困惑度接近Transformer的同时显著降低了计算和内存复杂度，为长上下文推理提供了可扩展的稀疏替代方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: Transformer在长上下文上二次缩放问题严重。该论文提出并行因果关联域（PCAF），通过并行内容寻址记忆机制，将上下文窗口中的局部记录写入哈希桶，为当前查询检索有限候选集，形成稀疏缓存分布，并与参数化语言模型通过门控混合。在长序列语言建模基准上，PCAF在保持困惑度接近Transformer的同时显著降低了计算和内存复杂度，为长上下文推理提供了可扩展的稀疏替代方案。

quick_skim.title: ReasonAlloc: Hierarchical Decoding-Time KV Cache Budget Allocation for Reasoning Models
quick_skim.abstract: Long chain-of-thought (CoT) trajectories in large language model (LLM) reasoning cause severe inference bottlenecks due to rapid key-value (KV) cache growth. Current decoding-time compression methods mitigate this issue via token eviction, but typically assume a uniform budget distribution across all layers and heads. In contrast, existing non-uniform budget allocation methods are predominantly designed for the static prompt prefill phase, and they do not capture the stepwise context demands of autoregressive reasoning. To bridge this gap, we propose ReasonAlloc, a training-free framework that recasts decoding-time KV compression as a hierarchical budget allocation problem. ReasonAlloc operates at two complementary levels: an offline layer-wise preallocation strategy captures an architecture-driven demand pattern which we call ``\textit{Reasoning Wave}'', while an online head-wise strategy reallocates resources during decoding to information-rich heads based on real-time utility. Evaluations on mathematical reasoning benchmarks (MATH-500, AIME~2024) using DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-8B, DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-14B, and AceReason-14B show that ReasonAlloc outperforms uniform-budget R-KV, SnapKV, and Pyramid-RKV (a baseline enforcing a static, monotonically decreasing layer budget), with the largest gains at small budgets (128-512 tokens). ReasonAlloc is plug-and-play with existing token-eviction policies and introduces negligible inference-time overhead.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes hierarchical KV cache budget allocation during decoding for reasoning models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出面向推理模型的分层解码时KV缓存预算分配方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出面向推理模型的分层解码时KV缓存预算分配方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出面向推理模型的分层解码时KV缓存预算分配方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ReasonAlloc recasts decoding-time KV compression as hierarchical budget allocation, outperforming uniform methods for reasoning trails.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对推理模型长思维链导致的KV缓存增长瓶颈，提出ReasonAlloc，一个无需训练的分层预算分配框架。它包含离线层和在线头两个级别的分配策略，以捕捉自回归推理的逐步上下文需求。实验表明，ReasonAlloc在保持推理质量的同时显著降低了峰值KV缓存使用量，优于统一的逐token驱逐方法，为推理模型的解码优化提供了新范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对推理模型长思维链导致的KV缓存增长瓶颈，提出ReasonAlloc，一个无需训练的分层预算分配框架。它包含离线层和在线头两个级别的分配策略，以捕捉自回归推理的逐步上下文需求。实验表明，ReasonAlloc在保持推理质量的同时显著降低了峰值KV缓存使用量，优于统一的逐token驱逐方法，为推理模型的解码优化提供了新范式。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: What are the latest methods for KV cache reuse in LLM inference?

quick_skim.title: SpectrumKV: Per-Token Mixed-Precision KV Cache Transfer for Prefill-Decode Disaggregated LLM Serving
quick_skim.abstract: Prefill-decode (PD) disaggregation decouples prompt processing from token generation, but it also turns the key-value (KV) cache into a network payload. Existing PD-side KV reduction methods are mostly binary: selected tokens are transmitted at full precision and the rest are not transmitted. This paper argues that binary selection leaves a useful design space unused. SpectrumKV assigns a precision level to each token instead: attention sinks and other high-importance tokens are protected at FP16, medium-importance tokens are sent at INT8, and low-importance tokens are sent at INT4 when the model can tolerate it. The main practical complication is that INT4 tolerance is model-dependent. Qwen2.5-7B catastrophically fails under INT4 KV quantization, while Mistral-7B and Gemma-2-9B remain stable. SpectrumKV therefore runs a lightweight deployment-time probe: three aggressive NIAH trials under a 3-tier policy. Models that pass use FP16+INT8+INT4; models that fail fall back to FP16+INT8. Across Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3, and Gemma-2-9B-it, SpectrumKV improves quality at the same transfer budget. At a 50% normalized KV budget on WikiText-2, SpectrumKV changes perplexity by +1.97%,-0.06%, and-0.44%, respectively, compared with PDTrim's +25.85%, +22.07%, and +35.63%. On NIAH retrieval at 4096 tokens, the adaptive policy reaches 52.6% on Qwen at the aggressive b=0.3 budget versus 26.3% for PDTrim, and reaches 100% by b=0.5; Mistral and Gemma preserve retrieval under the 3-tier policy. End-to-end GPU timing of the transfer path shows 50-62% TTFT reductions at b=0.5. These results suggest that PD KV transfer should be treated as a precision-allocation problem, not only as token pruning.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: mixed-precision KV cache transfer for disaggregated serving
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向分离式服务的混合精度KV缓存传输
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向分离式服务的混合精度KV缓存传输
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向分离式服务的混合精度KV缓存传输
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces SpectrumKV that assigns per-token precision levels (FP16/INT8/INT4) for KV cache transmission in prefill-decode disaggregated LLM serving, improving transfer efficiency with minimal quality loss.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 预填充-解码分离将KV缓存变为网络负载，现有方法多为二元选择（全精度传输或不传）。本文提出SpectrumKV，为每个令牌分配精度级别：注意汇聚等高重要性令牌用FP16，中等用INT8，低重要性用INT4，从而充分利用设计空间。实验表明，在保持模型质量的同时，显著减少传输带宽。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 预填充-解码分离将KV缓存变为网络负载，现有方法多为二元选择（全精度传输或不传）。本文提出SpectrumKV，为每个令牌分配精度级别：注意汇聚等高重要性令牌用FP16，中等用INT8，低重要性用INT4，从而充分利用设计空间。实验表明，在保持模型质量的同时，显著减少传输带宽。

quick_skim.title: FadeMem: Distance-Aware Memory Consolidation for Autoregressive Video Diffusion
quick_skim.abstract: Autoregressive video generators synthesize long videos by generating successive temporal segments, but their historical KV cache grows with video length. Existing bounded-cache methods reduce this cost with local windows, sink tokens, or compressed memory states, yet they usually assign fixed roles to different parts of the history. We propose FadeMem, a distance-aware KV memory consolidation mechanism that organizes historical KV blocks into a temporal hierarchy under a fixed cache budget. This design is motivated by frequency-dependent temporal decay: fine details decorrelate quickly, while coarse scene structure and identity remain useful over longer horizons. During generation, new history is inserted as fine-grained entries, while older adjacent entries are progressively merged under a power-law temporal allocation schedule, yielding a dense-near, sparse-far memory within one cache. Without architectural changes, FadeMem preserves recent context for short-term dynamics and compact long-range anchors for identity and scene coherence. Experiments show improved subject consistency, background stability, and temporal coherence over existing bounded-cache strategies.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: distance-aware KV memory consolidation for long video generation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于长视频生成的距离感知KV记忆整合
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于长视频生成的距离感知KV记忆整合
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于长视频生成的距离感知KV记忆整合
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: FadeMem proposes a distance-aware KV memory consolidation mechanism for autoregressive video diffusion, organizing KV blocks into a temporal hierarchy to manage cache growth under a fixed budget, improving long video generation efficiency.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 自回归视频扩散中KV缓存随视频长度线性增长，现有固定预算缓存方法对历史各部分分配固定角色，无法适应时间依赖性。FadeMem提出距离感知KV记忆整合机制，将历史KV块按时间距离组织成层次结构，新历史作为细粒度条目插入，旧信息根据频率衰减降级。在固定缓存预算下，该方法有效管理了缓存增长，保持了生成质量并显著降低内存占用，为长时自回归生成任务提供了可扩展的缓存管理方案。
